metadata.summary: Webinar planning framework: format selection, content structure, multi-channel promotion timeline, registration page copy, slide outline. Start promotion 3 weeks before.

content.title: Webinar Planning and Promotion
content.summary: A structured framework for planning and promoting webinars to generate leads and demonstrate expertise. Covers format selection, content structure, multi-channel promotion timeline, registration page copy, and slide deck outline. The core rule: start promotion 3 weeks before the event with a multi-channel sequence; last-minute promotion is the single biggest cause of low attendance.

why.p: Prospects want to see you in action before committing. Webinars deliver 30-60 minutes of trust-building that no written asset matches. But most webinars fail at the promotion stage — generic topics attract nobody, and single-channel promotion (email only) caps registrations. A structured 3-week multi-channel plan (email + social + partners + retargeting) consistently reaches 3-10% registration rate on landing page visits.

list.item: Generating registration page copy and email promotion sequences for a planned webinar
Creating a full slide deck outline from a topic brief and defined audience
Drafting a multi-channel promotion timeline for a specific event date
Selecting the best webinar format (educational/demo/panel/workshop) for a given objective
Writing speaker/co-host outreach emails for panel or interview-style webinars
Picking the webinar topic — topic validation requires ICP interviews, not synthesis
Technical platform setup (Zoom/Demio/Livestorm account configuration)
Real-time webinar moderation or chat management
Estimating registrations without historical benchmark data from prior webinars
When the product has no established audience to promote to

section.name: webinar-format-and-planning
section.p: Format types, planning decisions, multi-channel promotion timeline, and registration page elements.

rule.statement: Select webinar format based on marketing objective before planning any content or promotion.
rule.rationale: Format determines conversion style: educational webinars nurture; demo webinars close; workshops create premium offers. Mismatched format and objective produce low post-webinar conversion.

list.item: Educational: lead generation, soft CTA, nurture path
Demo: product showcase, direct CTA to trial or purchase
Q&A/AMA: community building, relationship-first
Panel/Interview: authority building through association
Workshop: deep engagement, premium offer conversion
Launch event: product launches, urgency-based close

rule.statement: Lock topic, date, platform, and whether there is a co-host before requesting any copy from an agent.
rule.rationale: Changing these after drafting requires full regeneration of all assets; sequential locking prevents rework.

list.item: Duration: 45-60 min (30 min content + 15-30 min Q&A)
Timing: Tuesday-Thursday, 11am or 2pm in audience's primary timezone
Frequency: Monthly is sustainable for solo founders
Platforms: Zoom, Demio, Livestorm (Demio/Livestorm have better registration APIs)

rule.statement: Follow the 60-minute structure: 5 min welcome, 5 min hook, 25 min content, 5 min soft pitch, 15 min Q&A, 5 min close/CTA.
rule.rationale: Deviating from this structure — especially skipping the soft pitch before Q&A — leaves conversion on the table.

rule.statement: Start promotion 3 weeks before the event; multi-channel coverage is required — email-only promotion caps registrations.
rule.rationale: Single-channel (email-only) promotion consistently underperforms multi-channel by 3-5x in registrations.

list.item: 3 weeks before: announce, start email + social promotion
2 weeks before: guest post pitch, podcast appearance pitch, partner cross-promotion
1 week before: email reminder sequence starts (3 emails)
Day before: reminder email
Day of: final reminder 1 hour before start
After: replay email to registrants who did not attend

rule.statement: Registration form must have a maximum of 2 fields (email + name); each additional field reduces conversion by 20-30%.
rule.rationale: Friction on the registration page is the second most common cause of low signups after weak promotion.

list.item: Headline: problem statement (not "Join our webinar")
Date/time with timezone converter link
Host credibility: photo + 1-2 sentence bio
3-4 specific learning outcomes as bullets
Replay promise for those who cannot attend live
Registration rate: 3% of page visits (good), 10%+ (great)
Promotion email open rate: 25% (good), 40%+ (great)
Show-up rate: 30% of registrants live (typical), 45%+ (good)
Partner referrals: 10% of registrations (good), 30%+ (great)

section.name: templates-examples-and-patterns
section.p: Registration page copy template, slide deck structure, real performance examples, and agent prompt patterns.

section.name: templates
section.reference: Registration page copy with headline, benefit bullets, host bio, and CTA
20-slide structure with presenter note scaffolding per slide

example.title: Lead generation educational webinar — 'Five SEO Mistakes Killing Your Traffic'
example.code: Promotion: 3,000-subscriber email list + 3 social posts/channel + 1 partner email Results: 800 registrations, 350 live attendees (44%), 150 replay viewers, 50 trial signups (14% of total viewers)

example.title: Product launch demo webinar with urgency offer
example.code: Promotion: waitlist email + limited seats (urgency) + early bird discount Results: 500 registrations, 300 live attendees, $15,000 in launch sales, 30% of attendees purchased

antipattern.description: Generic topic with no specific problem statement (e.g., "How to grow your business").
antipattern.reason: Broad topics attract nobody with a pressing pain; specific problems ("5 mistakes killing your X") attract the exact ICP.

antipattern.description: Starting promotion fewer than 10 days before the event.
antipattern.reason: Multi-channel cadence requires 3 weeks; compressed timelines produce fewer than 30% of potential registrations.

antipattern.description: No partner outreach — relying only on owned channels.
antipattern.reason: Partner cross-promotions deliver 10-30% of registrations at zero cost; skipping them caps reach.

antipattern.description: Complex registration form (company, role, phone, more than 2 fields).
antipattern.reason: Each additional field reduces conversion by 20-30%; friction here is the second biggest cause of low signups.

rule.statement: Provide topic, audience, date/time, email list size, platform, and goal before requesting any webinar assets.
rule.rationale: Under-specified prompts produce placeholder copy that requires full rewrite; these 6 inputs produce deployable drafts.

example.title: Webinar campaign asset generation prompt

list.item: Prospects want to see you in action before committing; webinars deliver 30-60 minutes of trust-building that no written asset matches. This is why webinars outperform written content as a conversion mechanism.
Generic topics attract nobody with a pressing pain; specific problems attract the exact ICP. A title like "How to grow your business" fails; "5 SEO mistakes killing your traffic" works.

Multi-channel cadence requires 3 weeks minimum; compressed timelines produce fewer than 30% of potential registrations. The promotional window must span email, social, partnerships, and paid channels.

Partner cross-promotions deliver 10-30% of registrations at zero cost; skipping them caps reach. Identify 3-5 micro-influencers or complementary brands in your space and propose a cross-promotion 2 weeks before the event.
Each additional field on the registration form reduces conversion by 20-30%; friction here is the second biggest cause of low signups after weak promotion. Stick to email and name only.
Deviating from the 60-minute structure — especially skipping the soft pitch before Q&A — leaves conversion on the table. The structure (welcome, hook, content, pitch, Q&A, close) is battle-tested.
Single-channel (email-only) promotion consistently underperforms multi-channel by 3-5x in registrations. Mandatory channels: email list, social media (Twitter, LinkedIn), partner outreach.
Registration rate benchmarks: 3% of page visits is good; 10%+ is great. If below 2%, audit the headline and topic specificity first, then the promotion volume.
Show-up rate benchmarks: 30% of registrants live is typical; 45%+ is good. If below 25%, the registration-to-event delay is too long or reminder sequences are weak.
Topic validation is not an agent task; it requires ICP interviews. Agents can help structure and promote the webinar, not validate whether the topic resonates with your audience.

template.purpose: Full registration page copy structure with placeholders
20-slide webinar structure with presenter note scaffolding

see-also.link: ConvertKit — email and landing page platform
